rulings.Case Title: IN RE: THE KAREN RUSH REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: REPORT OF SALE AND PETITION TO CONFIRM SALE OF REAL PROPERTY (4676 Ming Avenue, Bakersfield, Ca 93309)
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. Counsel to address the below procedural issues: (1) Petition sets forth the 1st Overbid amount as $1,276,750; however, the correct amount should be $1,276,250, and (2) Bond amount to include 10% recovery amount. LRSF 14.47.B and CRC 7.207. Petition #5.c suggests additional bond of $1,155,000 to cover the net proceeds; however, $1,270,500 additional bond is needed to include 10% recovery bond ($1,155,000+$115,500 = $1,270,500). PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties may appear personally or remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 160 225 4765 and password 514879. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252 and key in meeting ID "160 225 4765#", then participant ID "0#", then password "514879#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appearing by video input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Counsel or parties appearing in person must wear a mask covering their nose and mouth at all times while in the courthouse. Please review LRSF 14.1(D) for more information.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JAMES WONG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/30/23. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: There was already a final report. This petition should have been simply a petition to distribute after-discovered assets if such a petition were needed. If this interest in real property was community property, as both the petitioner claims and the I&A shows, the surviving spouse could have simply filed a Spousal Property Petition. Did the decedent and his wife own it as joint tenants with their daughter or as tenants in common? If joint tenants, the property passes to the surviving joint tenants by operation of law: the wife and daughter. A copy of the vesting need might need to be filed to confirm how title to this property is held. Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 1/20/23 if the petition is not dismissed. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ILENE BRETHOLZ
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/18/23. (No earlier availability.) Issues necessitating continuance: Due to a shortage of court clerks, the second amended petition, while file stamped 11/3/22, was not actually e-filed until 11/4/22, when it was first available to be viewed. Notice is defective if mailed before the filing (e-filing) of the pleading requiring notice. Is the administrator not seeking reimbursement of the amounts shown on Schedule F? This is missing from the petition caption and prayer. [CRC 7.102.] Exhibit D to the second amended petition has two charts showing the incorrect distribution calculation. (Why attach the amended distribution calculation filed on 8/9/22 when the amounts were - or should have been - changed in this second amended petition?) All attorney costs must be set forth in a separate attorney declaration, showing the date when each cost was incurred, who was paid, what the purpose of the payment was, and what each separate amount was. The narration in paragraph 23 is not helpful and none of those amounts (or all of those amounts combined do not) seem to correspond to the attorney costs shown on Schedule C (Disbursements). The $1,000 "retainer" paid by the administrator on 12/4/19, prior to appointment, must be deducted from the attorney's statutory fee unless the administrator or her attorney can clearly show that it was expended for costs during administration. It is the duty of the personal representative to marshal assets and submit an accounting. The proposed order does not show the amounts distributable to each heir. [CRC 7.651 and LRSF 14.35 I.6.] (The receipt for the sale of the jewelry is illegible. The administrator was appointed on 9/28/20; the order was not filed until 9/29/20. A personal representative is never discharged in an order for final distribution; the mandatory Judicial Council form DE-295 must be submitted after distribution is completed.) A courtesy copy of an amendment to the second amended petition and of the proof of service of notice of the amendment showing that notice was mailed after it was e-filed and a revised proposed order must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 1/9/23. PLEASE DO NOT FILE A THIRD AMENDED PETITION. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JERRY STEVEN PUCCIO
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR PROBATE OF WILL AND FOR LETTERS TESTAMENTARY
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 1/17/2023 for counsel to clear the following procedural defects: 1. Notice of Hearing and Publication must state "Lost Will" being offered. CRC 7.50 and LRSF 14.20; 2. Confidential Statement needs to be filed pursuant to LRSF 14.18.E; 3. Proposed Order must include a copy of the Lost Will is to be admitted pursuant to PrC 8223; 4. Petition #5 to be completed (3)-(4) re: registered domestic partner and (7)-(8) re: issue of predeceased child; 5. Petition #8 must set forth all heirs at law. Need to address if Daniel, predeceased sibling, was survived by issues. If so, said heirs must be noticed; and 6. further allegation to overcome Prob. Code 6124 presumption. d The drafting attorney's declaration does not appear sufficient to show the decedent's will was in his possession at the time of his death, and the will was drafted five years prior to the death. Further supporting allegations needed.
